evidence: $50.0 million minority investment in Bolt Data Energy, Inc. pursuant to a strategic agreement to develop and enable large scale data center campuses and supporting infrastructure across our land.
Bolt is a data energy infrastructure company co-founded by Eric Schmidt, former CEO and Chairman of Google.
Strategic agreement to develop and enable large scale data center campuses and supporting infrastructure across our land.
We received an equity interest, warrants, and a right of first refusal to supply water to Bolt-affiliated projects and related infrastructure.
Projects include evaluation of data centers, power generation, grid-connected batteries, and carbon capture and sequestration, among other opportunities.
Continually evaluate emerging threats and improved mitigation methods, including but not limited to, generative and agentic artificial intelligence and machine learning.
Minority investments inherently involve a lesser degree of control over business operations, thereby potentially increasing the financial, legal, operational and/or compliance risks.

capex_mentions.quote: $50.0 million in Bolt pursuant to a strategic agreement to develop and enable large scale data center campuses
capex_mentions.context: December 2025 minority equity investment in Bolt Data Energy, Inc. for data center infrastructure on TPL land

limitations: AI investment intensity: the $50M Bolt investment is primarily a data center infrastructure play; AI is not explicitly named as the core purpose — data centers are AI-adjacent but the filing does not directly label this as an AI investment. Score reflects named initiative without explicit AI-attributable quantification.
Narrative concreteness: named initiative and use cases described but no deployment metrics or quantified business results disclosed; projects described as in feasibility/development stage.

Risk disclosure: risk-section material is present; AI is mentioned only in the cybersecurity context as an emerging threat to monitor, not as a company-specific operational or strategic risk scenario with detailed mechanisms. Minority investment risks are named but not AI-specific.
